title: What is Ditto Scan?
content: Ditto Scan is a folder-based slideshow app for Android that is especially well suited to tablets. It lets you turn local folders into reusable fullscreen slideshows for photos, videos, and optional background music.

title: How do I choose media for a slideshow?
content: You pick a local folder using Android's document picker. Ditto Scan saves access to that folder so you can reopen it later without going through setup again each time.

title: Can I save multiple slideshow folders?
content: Yes. Each selected folder becomes its own saved slideshow profile, so you can keep separate setups for different albums, events, or collections.

title: Can photos and videos play together?
content: Yes. Ditto Scan supports mixed media playback, so photos and videos can play together in one continuous fullscreen slideshow.

title: Can I add music to the slideshow?
content: Yes. If the selected folder includes supported audio files, Ditto Scan can use them as optional background music during playback.

title: Can videos use their own sound?
content: Yes. You can choose whether videos play with their own audio or stay muted while background music continues.

title: What settings are saved for each folder?
content: Each folder can keep its own photo duration, sort mode, subfolder preference, video audio behavior, and fit or fill screen mode.

title: Can Ditto Scan scan subfolders too?
content: Yes. When you add a folder, you can decide whether Ditto Scan should include media from subfolders during scanning.

title: Is Ditto Scan designed for tablets?
content: Yes. The app uses a tablet-friendly home screen layout with roomier grids, touch-friendly cards, and an easy way to reopen saved slideshow folders.

title: Do I need to upload my media to the cloud?
content: No. Ditto Scan is built around local folders you explicitly choose on your device, so your media can stay private and local.
